meaning: adj.从拉丁文衍生的,类似拉丁文的
examples: The use of latinate words adds a tone of severity and dignity to this novel.
Most Latinate English words appeared with the French of the Norman Conquest of 1066 or appeared even later, introduced by people thinking they were smart to drop Latin based words into their writing.
Microsoft's lawyer, John Warden, is an earthy figure with a bit a southern twang who peppers his folksy zingers with Latinate legalese.
He likes to superimpose a trelliswork of latinate formulations (grids of the apodictic, the epistemological, the asymptotic) upon the subject at hand.
